name: Melatonin

dosing: Most commonly reported dose is 10 mg at bedtime, with a typical range of 9-25 mg during active cycles.
Some users take lower maintenance doses of 3-5 mg outside of cycle periods.
Higher doses up to 18 mg are used during severe cycles and tapered down during remission.
preparations: Standard oral tablets or capsules taken approximately 30 minutes before sleep.
Often used as a cofactor within the Vitamin D3 Anti-Inflammatory Regimen (Batch protocol).
When discontinuing, a gradual weaning protocol is recommended: 10 mg to 5 mg to 2.5 mg to stop.
schedule: Taken nightly at bedtime to prevent nocturnal attacks.
Higher doses during active cycles, reduced to a lower maintenance dose during remission.
Many users take it year-round as a preventive rather than only during active cycles.

sideEffects: Possible interaction with psilocybin/LSD busting via 5-HT2A receptor modulation
Reduced attack frequency without full elimination
